criteria.name: post_boot_terminal_welcome
criteria.description: After dismissing boot, ASCII YOUR NAME art appears above the two-column greeting, rocket, subtitle/email, Capabilities, and Navigation welcome box, followed by a focused command input

criteria.name: seeded_projects_and_shortcuts
criteria.description: Running /work or opening Projects Board shows at least six seeded case studies, and invoking a seeded shortcut such as /signals opens a detail block with type/year, blurb, tags, and stat chips

criteria.name: project_create_cross_view_flow
criteria.description: Starting from the seeded project count, creating Northline Atlas with valid name, blurb, status, tags, and stats increases the count by exactly one; it then appears in /work, Projects Board, its detail surface, matching filters, /stats, and the Portfolio JSON export preview without reload

criteria.name: project_edit_cross_view_flow
criteria.description: Editing one project to Evening Terminal updates that same record in Projects Board, /work output, its shortcut detail, tags/status filters, /stats when tags change, the export preview, and derived count without recreating or duplicating it

criteria.name: project_delete_cross_view_flow
criteria.description: Deleting a selected project decreases the measured count by exactly one and removes it from Projects Board, /work output, shortcut lookup, every matching filter, /stats, and the export preview on the first try

criteria.name: projects_empty_state
criteria.description: After deleting every active project through visible controls, Projects Board and /work show a meaningful empty-project state rather than a blank body, and /stats shows zero active projects

criteria.name: project_inline_validation
criteria.description: Submitting project create or edit with an empty required name, illegal slug, status outside shipped|wip|archived, tags outside 1–8 unique entries, or year outside 1990–2100 shows an inline field-specific error naming the offending field before submission and leaves the measured project count and last valid record unchanged

criteria.name: terminal_board_mode_switch
criteria.description: Switching between Terminal CLI and Projects Board through /work board or the visible mode control replaces the terminal body without a document reload or URL navigation

criteria.name: session_theme_commands
criteria.description: Running /dark, /light, /retro, and /glass applies four visibly distinct root-level themes without reload, while /themes lists the choices

criteria.name: boot_dismissal_paths
criteria.description: On a fresh load, sequential boot status lines end in a ready line and Press Enter to continue; Enter, pointer click, and touch-capable activation each dismiss boot into the terminal

criteria.name: command_catalog_and_clear
criteria.description: Slash commands echo their input and produce matching staggered output for /help, /about, /work, /clients, /skills, /philosophy, /social, /articles, /testimonials, /awards, /contact, /privacy, /stats, /export, /import, /archive, /undo, and /redo, while /clear wipes prior output

criteria.name: discoverable_easter_eggs
criteria.description: /secrets or /easter-eggs lists working hidden commands; /matrix starts green rain, /konami or the keyboard code triggers confetti, sudo hire designer reaches 100%, and the listed shell-themed extras print themed output

criteria.name: autocomplete_history_and_aliases
criteria.description: Typing a partial command opens matching suggestions, Tab completes the highlighted command, arrow keys traverse history, and an alias, bare-word fuzzy match, and natural-language phrase each resolve to their intended command

criteria.name: window_chrome_controls
criteria.description: Red close opens the kill-style exit overlay and Reopen restores the terminal; yellow minimize switches from blurred to sharp wallpaper; green maximize expands the terminal

criteria.name: cookie_consent_privacy_flow
criteria.description: After boot, choosing Accept or Decline dismisses the consent banner without an exception and /privacy reports the selected in-memory status; a fresh reload coherently returns consent to not-set; Portfolio JSON export consent matches /privacy

criteria.name: quick_info_inert_controls
criteria.description: /linkedin, /facebook, /instagram, /phone, /email, /agency, and /location print placeholder values inline, and all social, contact, award, and exit-overlay controls keep the current URL and tab unchanged

criteria.name: section_title_without_navigation
criteria.description: Running multiple section commands changes the document title to the active section while the URL remains unchanged and the single-page terminal stays mounted

criteria.name: filter_flow_round_trip
criteria.description: Applying a status or tag filter in Projects Board recomputes the visible cards from the shared collection, clearing the filter restores the full set exactly, and editing a project's status moves it between the corresponding filter results without a reload

criteria.name: theme_mode_state_continuity
criteria.description: Running /retro then toggling into Projects Board keeps the retro theme applied, switching back to Terminal CLI preserves the prior output buffer and up-arrow command history, and no step in the chain reloads the document

criteria.name: unknown_command_feedback
criteria.description: Typing an unrecognized command echoes the input and prints a command-not-found style message pointing at /help, while existing output stays intact and no exception is thrown

criteria.name: clear_preserves_session_state
criteria.description: After /clear wipes prior output, the prompt remains usable, pressing the up arrow still recalls earlier commands, and a subsequent /work still lists the full seeded project collection

criteria.name: double_submit_single_create
criteria.description: Double-activating the project create submit control adds exactly one project: the board card count increases by one and exactly one new entry appears in subsequent /work output

criteria.name: search_syntax_status_and_tag
criteria.description: In Projects Board or via /work, a search field accepts status:shipped / status:wip / status:archived and tag:Name syntax; applying a query recomputes visible projects, and clearing restores the full set exactly

criteria.name: syntax_matches_filter_controls
criteria.description: A status or tag filter control and a syntax query that express the same constraint show the same visible project set

criteria.name: stats_rollup_live_derived
criteria.description: /stats prints total count, per-status counts, and top tag frequencies derived from the current collection; creating, editing status/tags, deleting, bulk-updating, restoring, or undoing changes the figures on the next /stats run without reload

criteria.name: board_stats_strip_matches_stats
criteria.description: The Projects Board stats strip status counts always match the /stats numbers for the current active collection

criteria.name: bulk_select_and_actions_bar
criteria.description: In Projects Board each card/row has a checkbox; Select all checks every visible card; a bulk actions bar appears with the live selected count whenever at least one project is selected

criteria.name: bulk_archive_updates_surfaces
criteria.description: Selecting multiple projects and choosing Archive selected removes them from the board and /work, places them in /archive, drops /stats total by the selected count, clears selection, and removes them from the Portfolio JSON preview without reload

criteria.name: archive_restore_returns_project
criteria.description: /archive lists archived projects; Restore on a row returns that project to the active collection with prior fields, increases board card count by exactly one, and removes it from the vault

criteria.name: command_palette_fuzzy_activate
criteria.description: Ctrl+K (Cmd+K) opens a focused command palette; typing fuzzy-matches commands, projects, themes, and views; arrow keys move the highlight and Enter activates; Escape closes and returns focus to the command input

criteria.name: palette_kind_labels_and_empty
criteria.description: Each palette result row shows a kind label (Command, Project, Theme, or View); a query matching nothing shows an empty-state line rather than a blank list

criteria.name: undo_redo_project_mutations
criteria.description: Undo and Redo (toolbar, /undo /redo, and Ctrl+Z / Ctrl+Shift+Z) reverse and reapply create/edit/delete/bulk/archive/restore; empty stacks disable the controls; a new mutation after undo clears the redo stack

criteria.name: undo_restores_export_and_stats
criteria.description: After a project mutation, Undo restores the exact prior state across Projects Board, /work, shortcuts, filters, /stats, Archive vault, and the export preview

criteria.name: export_two_tabs_live_compile
criteria.description: /export or Export view shows Portfolio JSON and Resume Markdown tabs compiled live from session state; creating, editing, deleting, archiving, restoring, or undoing a project updates both tabs without reload

criteria.name: export_copy_and_download
criteria.description: Copy export puts the exact visible preview text on the clipboard with visible confirmation; Download offers the active tab as portfolio.json or resume.md

criteria.name: export_counts_match_stats
criteria.description: Portfolio JSON project count and status tallies match /stats and the board stats strip for the current session

criteria.name: import_valid_portfolio_json
criteria.description: /import or Export Import accepts a valid Portfolio JSON payload and replaces the active projects collection so board, /work, /stats, and export preview match the imported projects

criteria.name: import_export_round_trip_parity
criteria.description: Exporting current portfolio JSON and immediately re-importing that same JSON reconstructs the same visible project names, statuses, tags, slugs, and stats on the board and in /work

criteria.name: portfolio_json_api_shaped_field_contract
criteria.description: The Portfolio JSON export preview is a single object whose visible text includes the required field contract: version exactly "1.0"; theme exactly one of dark, light, retro, or glass; consent exactly one of accepted, declined, or not-set; and a projects array whose entries show name, slug, blurb, status (shipped|wip|archived), tags, stats, type, and year

criteria.name: created_project_record_matches_portfolio_json
criteria.description: After creating a project through the form with valid name, slug, blurb, status, tags, stats, type, and year, the Portfolio JSON projects[] entry for that project shows the same field names and values the form produced — the created record is the export payload

criteria.name: empty_projects_state_with_create_hint
criteria.description: After deleting every active project, Projects Board and /work output both render an empty-projects state that hints how to create one.

criteria.name: create_form_inline_validation_before_submit
criteria.description: On the project create form or command path, invalid project field-contract values (empty name, illegal slug, status outside shipped|wip|archived, tags outside 1–8, year outside 1990–2100) show inline per-field error messages naming the offending field before submit; submit stays disabled or rejects while any field fails the contract.

criteria.name: validation_errors_name_field_and_fix
criteria.description: Project create/edit validation messages name the problem and the fix (for example which field is empty) rather than only saying Invalid.

criteria.name: unknown_command_points_to_help
criteria.description: Typing an unrecognized command echoes the input and prints a command-not-found style message pointing at /help, without throwing or clearing existing output.

criteria.name: cli_thinking_indicator_before_output
criteria.description: Running a section command such as /about shows a CLI thinking indicator before the staggered output block appears.

criteria.name: exit_overlay_offers_reopen
criteria.description: Closing via the red titlebar control opens the exit overlay with a visible Reopen terminal path that restores the window without requiring a page reload.

criteria.name: help_lists_grouped_commands
criteria.description: /help lists available commands grouped by category so a new user can discover section, project, theme, export, archive, undo, and utility commands.

criteria.name: interactive_controls_use_real_buttons_inputs
criteria.description: Command input, autocomplete rows, board filters, checkboxes, bulk actions, banner Accept/Decline, titlebar dots, palette rows, and overlay CTAs use real button/input controls rather than inert non-interactive text alone.

criteria.name: exit_overlay_dismissible_paths
criteria.description: While the exit overlay is open, Reopen (and keyboard dismissal if offered) closes it and restores the terminal; the overlay is not stuck permanently after red close.

criteria.name: sudo_hire_progress_feedback
criteria.description: Running the command sudo hire designer advances a fake-contract progress bar to 100% with visible progress feedback rather than an instantaneous silent jump with no intermediate state.

criteria.name: double_submit_creates_exactly_one
criteria.description: Double-activating the project create submit adds exactly one project: the board card count increases by one and exactly one new /work entry appears.

criteria.name: clear_keeps_prompt_history_and_seed
criteria.description: /clear wipes prior output but leaves the prompt, up-arrow command history recall, and the seeded project collection intact for a subsequent /work.

criteria.name: empty_undo_redo_disabled
criteria.description: Undo with an empty history and Redo with an empty redo stack are disabled controls; activating them does nothing and produces no console errors.

criteria.name: malformed_import_rejected
criteria.description: Importing a malformed Portfolio JSON payload shows a visible validation message naming the problem, changes no projects, and leaves /work and the board unchanged.

criteria.name: schema_invalid_portfolio_json_import_rejected
criteria.description: Importing parseable JSON that fails the Portfolio JSON field contract — missing required version/theme/consent/projects keys, version not exactly "1.0", theme outside dark|light|retro|glass, consent outside accepted|declined|not-set, or a project entry that breaks the project field contract — leaves the collection unchanged and shows validation naming the offending field.

criteria.name: empty_filter_results_state
criteria.description: A syntax query or filter that matches nothing shows an empty-results state on the board with a clear way to clear the query; clearing restores the full set.

criteria.name: empty_archive_after_last_restore
criteria.description: Restoring the last archived project leaves /archive showing an empty-vault hint while the restored project appears on the board.

criteria.name: copy_export_uses_visible_preview
criteria.description: Copying the export while the preview is mid-update copies exactly the text currently visible in the preview block.

criteria.name: terminal_spacing_matches_reference_rhythm
criteria.description: At desktop width, spacing between titlebar, ASCII art, welcome box columns, and the command prompt follows a consistent terminal rhythm matching the reference screenshots rather than arbitrary one-off gaps.

criteria.name: jetbrains_mono_throughout
criteria.description: Typography uses JetBrains Mono monospace throughout the terminal chrome and output, matching the spec rather than a mixed proportional stack.

criteria.name: desktop_composition_matches_reference
criteria.description: At 1440px, the full-bleed wallpaper with a centered macOS-chrome terminal titled designer@portfolio ~ /portfolio matches the reference overview composition within a small tolerance.

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: Specified state changes animate via the real UI path: boot line reveals, staggered command output, /skills bars filling, and board card enter/exit on create/delete.

criteria.name: responsive_stack_matches_spec
criteria.description: At ≤768px the terminal expands toward full width and the welcome box stacks to one column as specified, matching the reference's narrow-width behavior.

criteria.name: material_chrome_fits_terminal
criteria.description: Buttons, overlays, snackbars/tooltips, and dense board controls share one consistent treatment that fits the terminal chrome rather than reading as a separate default-browser kit.

criteria.name: output_hierarchy_clear
criteria.description: Command output shows clear hierarchy among echoed prompt lines, section headings, skill bars, award/testimonial rows, and project detail blocks with tag and stat chips.

criteria.name: hover_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Via real pointer hover, titlebar dots ease opacity, project cards/rows ease border-color and background, autocomplete rows highlight, and social/close buttons ease — matching the specified hover language.

criteria.name: theme_surfaces_match_variants
criteria.description: Dark (default), light, retro CRT, and glass theme surfaces show precise color/depth/border treatments distinct from each other per the design.

criteria.name: microinteractions_match_duration
criteria.description: Hover and press microinteractions on titlebar dots, board rows, and autocomplete suggestions use short ease transitions rather than instant snaps or multi-second delays.

criteria.name: export_archive_palette_match_spec
criteria.description: Export two-tab preview, archive vault rows with Restore, and the command palette overlay match the specified terminal-chrome treatments rather than unrelated modal styles.

criteria.name: board_bulk_and_stats_match_spec
criteria.description: Projects Board selection checkboxes, bulk actions bar, and stats strip follow the dense terminal board pattern described in the spec and reference composition.

criteria.name: easter_egg_delight_beyond_stubs
criteria.description: Hidden easter eggs feel finished and delightful: /matrix rain, /konami confetti, and sudo hire designer progress read as polished moments rather than unfinished stubs.

criteria.name: crt_and_glass_theme_craft
criteria.description: /retro reads as a convincing CRT look (scanline/glow feel) and /glass as translucent glass, going beyond a flat class rename with no visual character.

criteria.name: boot_sequence_narrative
criteria.description: On a fresh load, the sequential boot status lines ending in a ready prompt create a guided onboarding narrative into the portfolio shell.

criteria.name: ascii_art_and_pixel_rocket_craft
criteria.description: Post-boot ASCII YOUR NAME art and the pixel/ASCII rocket in the welcome box provide distinctive interactive graphic craft beyond plain text dump.

criteria.name: konami_and_shell_extras_as_alt_input
criteria.description: The Konami keyboard code and shell-themed extras (for example whoami, ls, git log) offer playful alternative input paths discoverable via /secrets.

criteria.name: session_theme_personalization
criteria.description: Session-scoped theme switching across dark, light, retro, and glass lets the user personalize the terminal look without reload.

criteria.name: designer_portfolio_narrative_arc
criteria.description: Section commands (/about, /philosophy, /work, /awards, /testimonials) read as one coherent designer-portfolio narrative rather than disconnected dumps.

criteria.name: macos_chrome_immersion
criteria.description: macOS-style traffic-light chrome, wallpaper depth (blurred/sharp minimize), and the kill-style exit overlay create immersive windowing beyond a bare terminal box.

criteria.name: natural_language_command_matching
criteria.description: Natural-language phrases resolve to the correct commands, adding intent matching beyond exact slash syntax alone.

criteria.name: dual_mode_cli_and_board_polish
criteria.description: Terminal CLI and Projects Board modes feel like one polished product with coherent shared projects rather than two disconnected demos.

criteria.name: portable_portfolio_package_delight
criteria.description: Optional: the live Portfolio JSON + Resume Markdown export package feels like a finished takeaway artifact a designer would actually download, beyond a static dump.

criteria.name: command_palette_craft
criteria.description: Optional: the Ctrl+K command palette feels keyboard-first and fast, with clear kind labels and empty-state craft beyond a bare filtered list.

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable enhancement beyond the spec that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.

criteria.name: terminal_headings_consistent_case
criteria.description: Where the app renders terminal headings, /help group titles, and section copy, they use one consistent capitalization convention and tone throughout.

criteria.name: command_descriptions_are_specific
criteria.description: Where /help renders command descriptions, they say what each command does in specific terms rather than generic labels like Submit or OK.

criteria.name: validation_copy_names_field_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders create/edit or import validation errors against the project or Portfolio JSON field contract, they name the problem and the fix (for example which field is empty, illegal, or out of enum), never a bare failure word alone.

criteria.name: empty_projects_copy_explains_create
criteria.description: Where the app renders the empty projects state in Projects Board or /work, the copy explains what belongs there and how to create a project.

criteria.name: section_copy_spelling_grammar
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or section copy (/about bio, /philosophy, testimonials, award rows), rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.

criteria.name: project_terminology_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for projects/case studies across /work, Projects Board, and detail blocks, terminology stays consistent rather than mixing unrelated nouns for the same concept.

criteria.name: stat_chips_and_percentages_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders project stat chips and /skills percentages, numeric formatting is consistent (same percent style and chip presentation).

criteria.name: privacy_status_copy_is_specific
criteria.description: Where /privacy renders consent status, it states accepted, declined, or not set specifically rather than a vague affirmation.

criteria.name: identity_details_are_placeholders
criteria.description: Where the app renders name, email, phone, or social values, they read as clearly placeholder identity details rather than real personal data.

criteria.name: export_and_undo_labels_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders export tab labels and undo history labels, they read Portfolio JSON / Resume Markdown and use specific verbs such as Created, Edited, Archived, or Restored rather than generic Done.

criteria.name: empty_archive_filter_palette_copy
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty archive, empty filter results, or empty palette query states, the copy explains what belongs there and how to recover.

criteria.name: create_project_updates_work_board_shortcut_export
criteria.description: Create flow: after boot, create a valid project named Northline Studio; the /work listing grows by exactly one entry, Projects Board shows one more card with that name and a status badge, the new project's slash shortcut immediately prints its detail block, /stats total increases by one, and Portfolio JSON contains the new name — all without a reload.

criteria.name: invalid_create_blocks_and_names_field
criteria.description: Create flow: submitting the project create form or command with an empty project name, illegal slug, or year outside 1990–2100 leaves the measured project count unchanged and shows immediate inline validation naming the offending field in the terminal output or form.

criteria.name: edit_project_echoes_board_work_detail_export
criteria.description: Edit flow: editing a seeded project's name or tags updates that same record on the Projects Board card, the /work list line, the project's shortcut detail block, and the export preview without a reload.

criteria.name: delete_project_removes_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Delete flow: deleting a project decreases the Projects Board card count by exactly one, removes its line from subsequent /work output, drops it from status/tag filter results and the export preview, and its former shortcut no longer prints a detail block.

criteria.name: theme_and_mode_preserve_buffer_history
criteria.description: Theme and mode flow: running /retro then toggling into Projects Board keeps the retro theme applied; switching back to Terminal CLI preserves the output buffer and up-arrow command history; none of these steps reloads the document.

criteria.name: last_delete_shows_empty_projects_state
criteria.description: Edge: after deleting every active project, Projects Board and /work output both show a clear empty-projects state with a hint for how to create one.

criteria.name: board_filter_clear_and_status_move
criteria.description: Filter flow: applying a status or tag filter or status:shipped syntax query in Projects Board recomputes the visible cards from the shared collection; clearing restores the full set exactly; editing a project's status moves it between the corresponding filter results.

criteria.name: window_chrome_minimize_maximize_round_trip
criteria.description: Yellow minimize swaps the blurred wallpaper for the sharp one and the terminal remains operable afterward; green maximize expands the terminal window and the command prompt stays usable.

criteria.name: exit_overlay_reopen_restores_terminal
criteria.description: Red close opens the exit overlay with kill-style lines and a Reopen terminal control; activating Reopen restores the terminal window so the prompt is usable again without a full page reload.

criteria.name: consent_and_privacy_status_round_trip
criteria.description: Consent flow: before any choice /privacy reads not set; choosing Accept dismisses the banner and a subsequent /privacy reads accepted; on a fresh load choosing Decline makes /privacy read declined; export Portfolio JSON consent matches.

criteria.name: reload_restores_seeded_baseline
criteria.description: Reload baseline: after creating or editing a project, archiving, switching theme/mode, and opening Export, reloading the page replays the boot sequence and /work lists exactly the seeded projects — session mutations are reverted.

criteria.name: bulk_archive_with_undo_flow
criteria.description: Bulk archive with undo: selecting three visible board projects and choosing Archive selected moves all three to /archive, drops /stats by three, and removes them from export; Ctrl+Z restores all three to the board, prior /stats, and Portfolio JSON.

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Export then import: create Northline Atlas with a valid project field-contract payload, confirm Portfolio JSON contains it under projects[] with required version/theme/consent/projects keys, copy the JSON, delete Northline Atlas, import that payload, and confirm Northline Atlas returns on the board and in /work with the same status, tags, slug, and stats.

criteria.name: artifact_field_contract_flow
criteria.description: Artifact field-contract flow: after creating or editing a project and switching to /retro with consent accepted, open Export and confirm Portfolio JSON shows version exactly "1.0", theme exactly "retro", consent exactly "accepted", and a projects entry whose name, slug, blurb, status, tags, stats, type, and year match the form values.

criteria.name: palette_opens_project_and_export
criteria.description: Palette navigation: Ctrl+K, type part of a seeded project name, Enter on the Project result opens that detail; reopen palette and choose Export view to open the export preview.

criteria.name: interactive_controls_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Every interactive control (command input, autocomplete rows, board cards and filters, banner Accept/Decline, titlebar dots, overlay CTAs) is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone, with a visible focus indicator.

criteria.name: exit_overlay_focus_trap_and_return
criteria.description: While the exit overlay is open, focus stays within it; dismissing/reopening via keyboard restores the terminal and returns focus so the command input can be used again.

criteria.name: decorative_canvases_hidden_from_a11y_tree
criteria.description: Matrix-rain and confetti canvases are decorative: they are hidden from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden or equivalent) and never trap focus or block the command input.

criteria.name: validation_uses_polite_live_region
criteria.description: Form and command validation messages are exposed to assistive technology via a polite live region as well as shown visually.

criteria.name: create_edit_fields_have_accessible_names
criteria.description: Project create and edit fields expose explicit labels or equivalent accessible names for every required field.

criteria.name: terminal_headings_logical_order
criteria.description: Where the terminal renders section headings in command output (for example /help groups or project detail headings), heading levels follow a logical order without skipping levels that break the outline.

criteria.name: main_landmark_or_terminal_region
criteria.description: The post-boot terminal exposes a main landmark or an equivalently labeled primary region wrapping the command output and prompt.

criteria.name: contrast_across_themes
criteria.description: Terminal text and primary controls meet usable contrast against their surfaces in dark, light, retro, and glass themes.

criteria.name: semantic_buttons_for_chrome_and_banner
criteria.description: Titlebar traffic lights, cookie-banner actions, board filters, and overlay CTAs use semantic button controls (or ARIA button roles) rather than unlabeled clickable divs.

criteria.name: reduced_motion_disables_stagger_and_canvas
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion emulated on a fresh load, staggered/enter boot animations and matrix/confetti canvas animations are disabled or substantially reduced while the shell remains operable.

criteria.name: autocomplete_keyboard_complete
criteria.description: The autocomplete dropdown is keyboard operable: arrow keys move the highlight and Tab or Enter completes the highlighted command without requiring the pointer.

criteria.name: palette_and_bulk_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: The command palette (Ctrl+K), undo/redo controls, board selection checkboxes, bulk actions, export Copy/Download, and archive Restore are reachable and operable with the keyboard alone with a visible focus indicator.

criteria.name: command_palette_focus_trap
criteria.description: While the command palette is open, focus stays within it; Escape closes it and returns focus to the command input.

criteria.name: cold_start_interactive_under_two_seconds
criteria.description: On a local cold load, the app becomes interactive within 2 seconds and the boot animation starts promptly.

criteria.name: console_clean_across_shell_flows
criteria.description: No console errors or warnings appear during boot, command entry, project CRUD, theme switching, mode switching, or easter eggs.

criteria.name: theme_and_mode_transitions_snappy
criteria.description: Theme switches via /dark /light /retro /glass and mode toggles between Terminal CLI and Projects Board respond without multi-second UI lag.

criteria.name: thinking_indicator_before_command_output
criteria.description: Section commands show a CLI thinking indicator during the short wait before staggered output, rather than freezing the shell with a blank body.

criteria.name: seeded_board_and_work_scroll_smooth
criteria.description: With the seeded project collection (at least six case studies), /work listing and Projects Board scrolling/filtering feel responsive without perceived lag.

criteria.name: ui_interactive_during_command_output
criteria.description: While staggered command output is revealing, the command input remains focusable and the shell does not freeze.

criteria.name: matrix_rain_smooth_with_responsive_input
criteria.description: While /matrix rain is animating, the canvas motion stays visually smooth and the command input remains responsive to typing.

criteria.name: rapid_commands_stay_responsive
criteria.description: Rapid repeated command entry stays responsive with no hangs, dropped keystrokes, or frozen output.

criteria.name: matrix_work_stops_on_clear_or_dismiss
criteria.description: Running /clear or dismissing the matrix effect stops its ongoing animation work so the shell remains light afterward.

criteria.name: boot_visible_immediately_on_load
criteria.description: On cold load the boot sequence is visible promptly rather than a prolonged blank wallpaper with no terminal chrome.

criteria.name: export_recompile_stays_responsive
criteria.description: After a project create, delete, or archive, the export preview recompiles promptly with no multi-second freeze of the shell or command input.

criteria.name: rapid_undo_redo_responsive
criteria.description: Rapid repeated undo/redo stays responsive with no hangs, dropped keystrokes, or frozen board/export surfaces.

criteria.name: terminal_wallpaper_composition
criteria.description: A full-bleed atmospheric wallpaper frames one centered terminal window with macOS traffic lights and the title designer@portfolio ~ /portfolio, reading as a terminal tool rather than a multi-section marketing page

criteria.name: terminal_content_hierarchy
criteria.description: JetBrains Mono styling, ASCII art, the two-column welcome box, echoed prompts, headings, skill bars, testimonials, awards, and project detail chips form a legible dense hierarchy inside the terminal

criteria.name: four_coherent_themes
criteria.description: Dark, light, retro, and glass themes restyle the complete terminal coherently; retro visibly uses CRT scanline/glow cues and glass uses translucent surfaces while text remains readable

criteria.name: material_style_dense_controls
criteria.description: Buttons, overlay controls, feedback banners or snackbars, tooltips, forms, and dense controls share a polished component treatment that fits the terminal chrome rather than browser-default styling

criteria.name: projects_board_visual_structure
criteria.description: Projects Board uses dense cards or rows with consistent status badges, tag chips, stat chips, aligned controls, and a clearly styled empty state inside the terminal body

criteria.name: overlay_visual_fidelity
criteria.description: The exit overlay changes titlebar text to an exit path and shows red kill lines plus reconnect controls, while matrix and confetti canvases visibly cover the viewport without displacing terminal layout

criteria.name: responsive_terminal_layout
criteria.description: At roughly 375px wide the terminal remains readable, welcome content and project rows adapt without clipped text or page-level horizontal overflow, and all prompt and overlay controls remain reachable

criteria.name: breakpoint_reflow_768_and_mobile
criteria.description: At 768px and below the terminal window expands toward full width and the two-column welcome box stacks into a single column, while Projects Board cards reflow from a multi-column grid at desktop widths to a single column at narrow widths without clipping card content

criteria.name: terminal_copy_quality
criteria.description: Terminal output, /help descriptions, and section copy use one consistent capitalization and tone, command descriptions state specifically what each command does, and error or validation messages name the problem and the fix rather than a bare failure word

criteria.name: export_and_palette_visual_structure
criteria.description: Export view shows a monospaced Portfolio JSON / Resume Markdown preview with Copy and Download affordances, and the command palette reads as a centered overlay with kind-labeled result rows.

criteria.name: bulk_bar_and_stats_strip_chrome
criteria.description: Projects Board includes a compact stats strip and a bulk actions bar that visually belong to the terminal chrome rather than a disconnected dashboard widget.

criteria.name: shared_state_surface_coherence
criteria.description: Project mutations, command history, output, autocomplete, theme, active mode, filters, and consent remain mutually coherent across every visible terminal and board surface during interleaved interactions

criteria.name: local_assets_and_theme_tokens
criteria.description: Computed styles expose the required theme-token behavior and locally rendered component styling, while the browser network log shows scripts, styles, fonts, icons, and animation assets loading from the app origin with no third-party CDN requests

criteria.name: in_memory_reload_reset
criteria.description: After creating and filtering a project, changing mode and theme, building command history, and setting consent, a full reload coherently resets all facets to the seeded session baseline with browser localStorage and sessionStorage empty

criteria.name: console_clean_primary_flows
criteria.description: Initial load and the boot, command, project CRUD, filter, theme, window, consent, form-validation, and easter-egg flows produce no uncaught exception, unhandled rejection, hydration-style mismatch warning, or failed same-origin asset request

criteria.name: single_page_route_coherence
criteria.description: Section-title changes, theme changes, mode switches, inert controls, and close/reopen behavior keep the same URL and mounted single-page state without a blank screen or content flash

criteria.name: form_error_state_integrity
criteria.description: Invalid and extreme project form input against the project field contract (empty name/slug, illegal slug characters, status outside shipped|wip|archived, tags outside 1–8, year outside 1990–2100) produces specific inline per-field feedback before submit, preserves the last valid state, and repeated identical submissions do not create duplicate records

criteria.name: keyboard_operability_and_focus
criteria.description: The command input, autocomplete rows (arrow keys move the highlight, Tab or Enter completes), board cards and filters, banner buttons, titlebar dots, and overlay CTAs are all reachable and operable with the keyboard alone with a visible focus indicator, and the exit overlay keeps focus within it while open and can be dismissed from the keyboard

criteria.name: live_region_and_decorative_canvases
criteria.description: Form and command validation messages are exposed to assistive technology through a polite live region as well as visually, while the matrix-rain and confetti canvases are hidden from the accessibility tree and leave keyboard focus and the command input unblocked

criteria.name: load_and_rapid_input_performance
criteria.description: The app is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load with boot starting promptly, rapid repeated command entry stays responsive with no hangs or dropped keystrokes, and the matrix-rain canvas animates while the command input remains responsive with /clear or dismissal stopping its work

criteria.name: undo_export_state_coherence
criteria.description: Undo and redo of a project mutation keep Projects Board, /work, /stats, archive, and export preview mutually coherent without reload.

criteria.name: import_replaces_shared_collection
criteria.description: Importing a valid Portfolio JSON replaces the active projects collection such that board, /work, /stats, and export preview all reflect the imported set from the same shared state.

criteria.name: project_form_validation_matches_field_contract
criteria.description: Project create/edit and Import enforce the declared field contracts in the UI: empty or illegal name/slug, status outside shipped|wip|archived, tags outside 1–8 unique entries, or year outside 1990–2100 are rejected with inline feedback naming the field; Import of JSON that fails required version/theme/consent/projects keys or closed enums is rejected with feedback naming the offending field and does not mutate projects

criteria.name: theme_consent_cross_field_match_export
criteria.description: Switching theme via /retro and choosing Accept on consent updates the Portfolio JSON theme field to retro and consent field to accepted without a reload — a cross-field contract between session state and the export payload

criteria.name: terminal_adapts_1440_to_375
criteria.description: At 1440px the terminal window is centered over the wallpaper with visible margins; resizing to 375px keeps the terminal usable with the command input visible and no broken overflow.

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets_banner_and_chrome
criteria.description: At 375px width, cookie-banner Accept/Decline, titlebar dots, and primary board/filter controls present tap targets at least 44px in at least one dimension.

criteria.name: monospace_type_readable_across_widths
criteria.description: JetBrains Mono terminal copy remains legible at both 1440px and 375px without collapsing into unreadably tiny glyphs.

criteria.name: no_clip_or_overflow_at_375
criteria.description: At 375px width, no terminal content, welcome box, or board card clips or overflows the viewport.

criteria.name: narrow_width_expands_terminal_window
criteria.description: At widths of 768px and below, the terminal window expands toward full width rather than remaining a narrow centered desktop card that leaves unusable side gutters.

criteria.name: welcome_box_stacks_at_768
criteria.description: At widths of 768px and below, the two-column post-boot welcome box stacks into a single column with Capabilities and Navigation still readable in order.

criteria.name: boot_dismissible_by_touch
criteria.description: On a fresh load, boot can be dismissed via touch-capable activation as well as Enter/click, revealing the post-boot terminal.

criteria.name: no_horizontal_scroll_at_375
criteria.description: At 375px width the page shows no horizontal scrolling.

criteria.name: board_grid_and_canvases_reflow
criteria.description: Projects Board cards reflow from a multi-column grid at desktop widths to a single column at narrow widths without clipping card content; matrix-rain and confetti canvases size to the viewport when active.

criteria.name: command_input_usable_at_narrow_widths
criteria.description: At 375px and 768px the command input remains visible and usable for typing a command after boot.

criteria.name: export_palette_usable_at_375
criteria.description: At 375px width the command palette, export preview, and archive vault remain fully visible and operable; previews scroll within their own containers rather than forcing page-level horizontal scroll.

criteria.name: bulk_bar_usable_at_narrow
criteria.description: At 768px and below the bulk actions bar and board stats strip remain usable without clipping action labels.

criteria.name: boot_and_post_boot_motion
criteria.description: On a fresh load through the real prompt controls, boot lines reveal sequentially and dismissal makes the ASCII art and welcome box fade or slide in before focus settles on the command prompt

criteria.name: cli_output_and_mode_motion
criteria.description: A visible thinking indicator precedes staggered command output, and switching between Terminal CLI and Projects Board updates in place with a perceptible transition rather than a reload

criteria.name: skill_bar_growth
criteria.description: Running /skills through the visible command prompt renders at least ten labeled bars whose widths visibly animate from zero to distinct target percentages

criteria.name: window_and_theme_feedback
criteria.description: Theme changes recolor in place, minimize crossfades blurred and sharp wallpaper, maximize visibly expands the terminal, and reopening the closed terminal restores it with coherent transition feedback

criteria.name: easter_egg_canvas_motion
criteria.description: Invoking /matrix produces continuous moving green character rain, /konami produces a confetti burst, and sudo hire designer visibly advances a progress bar to 100%

criteria.name: required_hover_microinteractions
criteria.description: Using the real pointer path, titlebar dots ease opacity, project cards or rows ease border and background, autocomplete rows highlight, and social and close-overlay buttons visibly ease on hover

criteria.name: reduced_motion_behavior
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion enabled before a fresh load, staggered entry and continuous canvas effects are disabled or substantially reduced while every resulting state remains usable

criteria.name: board_card_enter_exit_animation
criteria.description: Creating a project through the visible form or command animates its new card or row into the Projects Board, and deleting one through the visible control animates it out, rather than the board snapping rows in or out with no transition

criteria.name: feedback_message_transition
criteria.description: Validation errors and confirmation messages appear with a brief fade or slide transition rather than popping in with no motion

criteria.name: palette_and_export_motion
criteria.description: Via the real UI path, the command palette opens/closes with a brief opacity or scale transition, and Export Copy shows a short confirmation transition before resetting.

criteria.name: archive_board_row_exit
criteria.description: Archiving a selected project through the visible bulk or per-project control animates its card/row out of the Projects Board rather than snapping it away with no transition.

criteria.name: in_memory_multi_facet_reload_resets
criteria.description: Starting after boot: create a project named Northline Studio, switch theme with /retro, open Projects Board, Accept the cookie banner, archive one project, and open Export; then reload. All facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline (boot replays, /work shows only seeded projects, theme/mode/consent/archive/undo/export return to defaults) — never a mixed partial survival.

criteria.name: board_filter_derived_view_sensitivity
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: apply a status:shipped syntax query or status filter in Projects Board and confirm the visible card set changes meaningfully; clear the query/filter and confirm the full seeded set returns exactly.

criteria.name: edit_echoes_board_work_detail_export
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: edit a seeded project's name, then without reload confirm Projects Board, /work output, that project's shortcut detail, and the Portfolio JSON export preview all show the edited name.

criteria.name: create_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: measure the Projects Board card count, /work entry count, and /stats total immediately before and after creating one valid project; all three increase by exactly one with no off-by-one lag.

criteria.name: different_project_names_different_outputs
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: create two projects with different names (Northline Studio then Coral Relay); their board cards, /work lines, shortcut detail blocks, and Portfolio JSON entries differ according to those names.

criteria.name: interleaved_create_and_theme
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: start creating a project, run /retro to switch theme, finish creating the project, then open Projects Board and Export; the new project is present in board and Portfolio JSON, the retro theme remains applied, and neither flow corrupted the other.

criteria.name: empty_then_repopulate_projects
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete every active project until empty states show in Board and /work, then create one valid project; both surfaces leave the empty state and show exactly that one project, and /stats total equals 1.

criteria.name: bulk_archive_undo_restores_export
criteria.description: Full-pipeline probe: select three board projects, Archive selected, confirm they leave the board and export preview and appear in /archive with /stats down by three; press Ctrl+Z and confirm all three return to the board, /stats prior totals, and Portfolio JSON names.

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_probe
criteria.description: Round-trip: create Northline Atlas with a valid project field-contract payload, open Export and confirm Portfolio JSON contains Northline Atlas under projects[] with required version/theme/consent/projects keys, copy that JSON, delete Northline Atlas, import the copied JSON, and confirm Northline Atlas returns on the board and in /work with the same status, tags, slug, and stats.

criteria.name: artifact_field_contract_probe
criteria.description: Full-pipeline probe: create or edit a project, switch to /retro, Accept cookie consent, open Export, and confirm Portfolio JSON shows version exactly "1.0", theme exactly "retro", consent exactly "accepted", and a projects entry whose name, slug, blurb, status, tags, stats, type, and year match the form values.

criteria.name: schema_invalid_import_leaves_state
criteria.description: Edge-state: import a parseable JSON payload that fails the Portfolio JSON field contract (for example version "2.0" or status "draft"); board card count, /work listing, and export preview stay unchanged and a validation message names the offending field.

criteria.name: stats_sensitive_to_status_edit
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: edit one project's status from shipped to wip and confirm /stats shipped count decreases by one and wip increases by one without reload.
